citation: Oakden-Rayner L, Dunnmon J, Carneiro G, Re C. Hidden Stratification Causes Clinically Meaningful Failures in Machine Learning for Medical Imaging. Proc ACM Conf Health Inference Learn. 2020:151-159.
case_summary: Clinically important low-prevalence subsets can fail despite strong aggregate metrics.
